definitions.gloss_en: an extended movement action that allows a unit to move at greater distance at the cost of other actions
definitions.gloss_jp: {通|つう}{常|じょう}より{長|なが}い{距|きょ}{離|り}の{移|い}{動|どう}が{許|ゆる}されるが、{他|た}の{行|こう}{動|どう}が{制|せい}{限|げん}される{行|こう}{動|どう}
definitions.explanation_en: A movement action emphasizing distance over combat readiness. Units that march typically cannot attack in the same turn.
definitions.implicit_notes: 行軍 is a classical Japanese military term for troop movement over extended distances. The connotation is strategic movement (getting from A to B) rather than tactical engagement.
definitions.orthography_notes: 行軍: 行 (go/march) + 軍 (military unit/army). Classical Japanese military term documented in WWII-era writing. JWC board wargame glossary attests 行軍 as a standard term. Goo dictionary defines it as a general military word.
definitions.game_setting_notes: In miniature wargames, 行軍 typically allows a unit to move farther (e.g., double movement distance) but prevents charging or shooting in the same turn. Occurs in the 移動段階.

usage_jp: {行|こう}{軍|ぐん}する (to march); {行|こう}{軍|ぐん}した{ユニット|ゆにっと}は{射|しゃ}{撃|げき}できない (units that marched cannot shoot)
comparative_notes: 前進 (advance) is the shorter tactical movement. 行軍 is the extended strategic movement with associated restrictions.

examples.japanese: {行|こう}{軍|ぐん}したユニットは{射|しゃ}{撃|げき}{段|だん}{階|かい}に{攻|こう}{撃|げき}できない。
examples.english: Units that marched cannot attack during the shooting phase.

cross_references.note: 前進 — advance (shorter movement, contrasted with 行軍)
移動段階 — the phase in which 行軍 occurs
